Candidate actions: rotate_right/left would change the L shape but current orientation already matches the small right-side contour; left/right could improve alignment later but one safe immediate progress move is downward. Hard drop risks committing without finer adjustment; soft_drop advances toward landing while preserving flexibility next turn.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is L near the upper-middle, currently in a valid horizontal-right-foot orientation. Surface below at columns 4-6 is uneven but there is immediate vertical space under the piece; no urgent top-out threat. Goal is to place the L to improve the central stack and avoid creating overhangs before upcoming T,Z,O,I pieces. Rotating right to orientation 3 would make a vertical L that can likely slot cleaner into the central-left channel around columns 4-5 on subsequent moves, reducing the current horizontal protrusion. Left/right alone does not yet improve placement as much, and dropping now in current orientation would likely create a wider shelf. So best immediate setup is rotate_right.

Immediate threats are low because there is plenty of vertical space, so this turn should focus on positioning for a cleaner future placement rather than rushing downward. Candidate actions: hard_drop or soft_drop would commit before aligning well; no_op wastes a move; rotating could prepare alternative fits but there is no urgent need yet. This advances the sub-goal of setting up a useful T placement without immediate risk.
